findings.title: Prototype pollution: deepmerge library
findings.description:
[COMPOUND: 3 co-located issues] Object merge/extend operation with user-controlled input can pollute Object.prototype.

**Analysis:**
🔍 Pattern: deepmerge library
⚠️ User input from: `JSON.parse`
📦 In function: `runMcpInit` (0 callers)
findings.why_it_matters:
Prototype pollution allows attackers to inject properties into Object.prototype, which affects ALL objects in the application. This can lead to:
• Remote Code Execution (via gadget chains)
• Denial of Service
• Authentication bypass
• Property injection

findings.title: Insecure `Math.random()` used for cryptographic operation
findings.description:
`Math.random()` is not cryptographically secure and can be predicted by attackers.

**Analysis:**
🎯 Used for: cryptographic operation
📦 In function: `pick`
findings.why_it_matters: Insecure random number generators (like Math.random or random.random) use predictable algorithms. Attackers can often guess the output and forge tokens, guess passwords, or bypass authentication.

findings.title: Potential SQL Injection (CWE-89)
findings.description:
**Potential SQL Injection Vulnerability**

**Pattern detected**: JavaScript template literal with interpolation in SQL query

**Location**: packages/shadcn/src/preset/presets.ts:174

**Code snippet**:
```javascript
type: "select",
```

SQL injection occurs when untrusted input is incorporated into SQL queries without
proper sanitization. An attacker could manipulate the query to:
- Access unauthorized data
- Modify or delete database records
- Execute administrative operations
- In some cases, execute operating system commands

This vulnerability is classified as **CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special
Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')**.
findings.why_it_matters: SQL injection is one of the most dangerous vulnerabilities, allowing attackers to access, modify, or delete sensitive data in the database.

findings.title: Async call without await
findings.description:
Async function called without await - returns Promise/coroutine, not the actual value.

**Analysis:**
📦 In async function: `BlocksPage`
🔍 Calls a function defined as async in this codebase
findings.suggested_fix: Add `await` before the async call.
findings.why_it_matters: Without await, you get a Promise object instead of the actual result.

findings.title: Structural bridge: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/ui/apps/v4/app/(app)/examples/rtl/components/index.tsx::RtlComponentsContent:29` separates components of [4449, 37]
findings.description: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/ui/apps/v4/app/(app)/examples/rtl/components/index.tsx::RtlComponentsContent:29` is a structural bridge. Removing it would split the graph into components of sizes [4449, 37]. All communication between these components currently flows through this single node.
findings.suggested_fix: Reduce coupling through this node by introducing alternative call paths, extracting shared interfaces, or splitting the bridge function into independently accessible units.
findings.why_it_matters: A structural bridge is a single node whose failure disconnects entire subsystems. This creates fragile architecture where a bug, API change, or refactor in one function can cascade to all dependent components.

findings.title: XSS: React dangerouslySetInnerHTML
findings.description:
[COMPOUND: 3 co-located issues] **dangerouslySetInnerHTML can introduce XSS vulnerabilities**

**Location**: apps/v4/app/(create)/components/action-menu.tsx:67

**Code snippet**:
```
dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{
```

Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerabilities occur when untrusted data is included
in web pages without proper validation or escaping. Attackers can inject malicious
scripts that:
- Steal user session cookies
- Capture keystrokes and credentials
- Redirect users to malicious sites
- Deface the application

This vulnerability is classified as **CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of
Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')**.
findings.suggested_fix:
**Recommended fixes**: 1. **Avoid dangerouslySetInnerHTML when possible**: ```jsx // Instead of:
// Use React's built-in escaping:
{userContent}
``` 2. **If HTML rendering is required**, sanitize first: ```jsx import DOMPurify from 'dompurify';
```
findings.why_it_matters: XSS vulnerabilities allow attackers to execute scripts in users' browsers, potentially stealing sensitive data or hijacking user sessions.

findings.title: Hook called inside a loop: `useDesignSystemSearchParams`
findings.description:
[COMPOUND: 2 co-located issues] React hooks must be called in the exact same order on every render.

**Analysis:**
🪝 Hook: `useDesignSystemSearchParams`
📦 Component: `DesignSystemProvider`
⚠️ Called inside `for/while/map/forEach`
findings.why_it_matters:
This violates the Rules of Hooks. React relies on the order of hook calls to track state correctly. Conditional/loop/nested hooks cause:
• State getting out of sync
• Crashes and rendering bugs
• Unpredictable behavior

findings.title: Empty catch block swallows exceptions
findings.description:
[COMPOUND: 3 co-located issues] This catch block silently swallows exceptions, hiding potential bugs.

**Risk Assessment:**
⚠️ `querySelector` appears to do I/O
findings.suggested_fix:
This swallows I/O or network exceptions - very dangerous!
At minimum, log the exception:
```python
except Exception as e:
logger.error(f"Operation failed: {e}")
```
findings.why_it_matters: Swallowed exceptions hide bugs and make debugging extremely difficult. When something fails silently, you may not know until much later.

[COMPOUND: 2 co-located issues] **dangerouslySetInnerHTML can introduce XSS vulnerabilities**

**Location**: apps/v4/components/component-source.tsx:111

**Code snippet**:
```
<div dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: highlightedCode }} />
```

Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerabilities occur when untrusted data is included
in web pages without proper validation or escaping. Attackers can inject malicious
scripts that:
- Steal user session cookies
- Capture keystrokes and credentials
- Redirect users to malicious sites
- Deface the application

This vulnerability is classified as **CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of
Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')**.
findings.suggested_fix:
**Recommended fixes**: 1. **Avoid dangerouslySetInnerHTML when possible**: ```jsx // Instead of:
// Use React's built-in escaping:
{userContent}
``` 2. **If HTML rendering is required**, sanitize first: ```jsx import DOMPurify from 'dompurify';
```
findings.why_it_matters: XSS vulnerabilities allow attackers to execute scripts in users' browsers, potentially stealing sensitive data or hijacking user sessions.

findings.title: Hook called inside a loop: `useIsMobile`
findings.description:
[COMPOUND: 2 co-located issues] React hooks must be called in the exact same order on every render.

**Analysis:**
🪝 Hook: `useIsMobile`
📦 Component: `TableCellViewer`
⚠️ Called inside `for/while/map/forEach`
findings.why_it_matters:
This violates the Rules of Hooks. React relies on the order of hook calls to track state correctly. Conditional/loop/nested hooks cause:
• State getting out of sync
• Crashes and rendering bugs
• Unpredictable behavior

findings.title: Empty catch block swallows exceptions
findings.description:
[COMPOUND: 3 co-located issues] This catch block silently swallows exceptions, hiding potential bugs.

**Risk Assessment:**
⚠️ `readFile` appears to do I/O
findings.suggested_fix:
This swallows I/O or network exceptions - very dangerous!
At minimum, log the exception:
```python
except Exception as e:
logger.error(f"Operation failed: {e}")
```
findings.why_it_matters: Swallowed exceptions hide bugs and make debugging extremely difficult. When something fails silently, you may not know until much later.

findings.title: Test code in production: 1 issue
findings.description:
Test utilities and patterns found in what appears to be production code.

**Found:**
• Mock/stub objects (line 508)
⚠️ This file is imported by production code!
findings.suggested_fix:
Options:

1. **Move to test files:** If this is test code, move it to a test directory
2. **Use TYPE_CHECKING:** For type hints only, wrap imports in `if TYPE_CHECKING:`
3. **Environment check:** If needed at runtime, guard with environment variable

```python
# Instead of:
from unittest.mock import Mock

# Use:
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
if TYPE_CHECKING:
from unittest.mock import Mock
```
findings.why_it_matters: This test code is imported by production code. Test dependencies add bloat, security risks, and can cause unexpected behavior in production.

findings.title: Potential ReDoS vulnerability
findings.description:
[COMPOUND: 2 co-located issues] Regex with nested quantifiers may cause catastrophic backtracking, leading to denial of service.

**Analysis:**
🔍 Pattern issue: nested quantifiers detected
📝 Pattern: `^(?:\^|~)?3(?:\.\d+)*(?:-.*)?$`
📦 In function: `getTailwindVersion` (3 callers)
findings.why_it_matters:
ReDoS attacks exploit regex backtracking to consume CPU:
• A malicious input can take exponential time to evaluate
• Single requests can freeze the server
• Input like 'aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa!' on /(a+)+$/ can hang

findings.suggested_fix: Add `await` before the async call.
findings.why_it_matters: Without await, you get a Promise object instead of the actual result.

In function: `updateCssPlugin`

1. Extract more nested blocks into similar helpers
2. Use guard clauses (early returns) to reduce nesting
3. Replace nested ifs with switch/match
findings.why_it_matters: Deep nesting makes code hard to read and maintain. Each level increases cognitive load exponentially.

findings.title: Broad exception catch
findings.description:
Catching generic Exception hides bugs and makes debugging difficult.

**Analysis:**
📞 Try block calls: buildRegistryBase, track, json, get, parseDesignSystemConfig
findings.suggested_fix:
Based on the operations in your try block, consider catching:
  • SyntaxError (for JSON.parse)
  • TypeError (network errors), AbortError

Example:
```python
except (SyntaxError (for JSON.parse)) as e:
logger.error(f"Operation failed: {e}")
```
findings.why_it_matters: Broad exception catches mask unexpected errors like TypeErrors or AttributeErrors that indicate bugs in your code.

findings.title: Hardcoded timeout: 2 seconds
findings.description:
Magic timeout value `2000` makes configuration and tuning difficult.

**Analysis:**
⏱️ Duration: 2 seconds
📍 Context: General timeout
📊 Same value used 4 times
📦 In function: `CarouselPlugin`
findings.suggested_fix:
This timeout value appears 4 times. Extract to a centralized config:

```python
# config.py
TIMEOUT_2000MS = 2000  # 2 seconds

# usage
from config import TIMEOUT_2000MS
requests.get(url, timeout=TIMEOUT_2000MS/1000)
```
findings.why_it_matters: Hardcoded timeouts are hard to find, tune, and change across environments. Network timeouts especially need to be configurable based on deployment.

findings.title: String concatenation in loop
findings.description:
Variable 'index' concatenated 2 times inside loop (started line 52).

**Performance:** O(n²) time complexity. Each concatenation creates a new string and copies all previous characters.

For 1000 iterations, this copies ~500,000 characters instead of 1000.
findings.why_it_matters: String concatenation in loops creates O(n²) time complexity due to immutable string copying.

findings.title: Regex compiled inside loop
findings.description:
Regex compiled in loop (loop starts line 47).

**Performance impact:** Regex compilation is expensive. In a loop of N iterations, this costs N compilations instead of 1.
findings.suggested_fix:
Move regex compilation outside the loop:
```rust
let re = Regex::new(pattern)?;  // Before loop
for item in items {
re.is_match(item);  // Reuse
}
```
findings.why_it_matters: Regex compilation involves parsing and optimization. Doing it once instead of N times can dramatically improve performance.

findings.title: String concatenation in loop
findings.description:
Variable 'newAttrs' concatenated 2 times inside loop (started line 142).

**Performance:** O(n²) time complexity. Each concatenation creates a new string and copies all previous characters.

For 1000 iterations, this copies ~500,000 characters instead of 1000.
findings.why_it_matters: String concatenation in loops creates O(n²) time complexity due to immutable string copying.

findings.title: Regex compiled inside loop
findings.description:
Regex compiled in loop (loop starts line 66).

**Performance impact:** Regex compilation is expensive. In a loop of N iterations, this costs N compilations instead of 1.
findings.suggested_fix:
Move regex compilation outside the loop:
```rust
let re = Regex::new(pattern)?;  // Before loop
for item in items {
re.is_match(item);  // Reuse
}
```
findings.why_it_matters: Regex compilation involves parsing and optimization. Doing it once instead of N times can dramatically improve performance.

findings.description:
== performs type coercion which can cause subtle bugs.

**Analysis:**
📦 In function: `FieldError` (1 callers)
findings.why_it_matters:
Type coercion in == can cause unexpected behavior:
• '1' == 1 is true
• '' == false is true
• [] == false is true
Use === to compare both value AND type.
